new_classes.definition_en: A beverage as defined in Chapter 22 Note 3 that contains no more than 0.5% alcohol by volume, intended for direct consumption, and not classified as a fruit or vegetable juice of heading 2009. Includes sweetened or flavoured waters, mineral waters, aerated waters with added sugar or flavouring, and other non-alcoholic beverages.

Water, including mineral water and aerated water, to which sugar, other sweetening matter, or flavouring has been added, classified under CN heading 2202. Excludes plain mineral water without additives and fruit or vegetable juices of heading 2009.

coverage_explanation: The proposed axioms cover the main distinguishing criteria for CN heading 2202: (1) non-alcoholic character (≤ 0.5% ABV via existing alcoholByVolumePercent property), (2) suitability for direct consumption as a beverage (via existing isForDirectConsumptionAsBeverage property), and (3) the specific sub-type of sweetened/flavoured/aerated water. The exclusion of fruit and vegetable juices of heading 2009 is not formally captured as a complement/disjointness axiom because a FruitOrVegetableJuice class is not present in the TBox. Additionally, the Chapter 22 Note 3 definition of 'non-alcoholic beverages' (which typically sets the 0.5% ABV threshold) is applied via the existing property but the note individual itself is not referenced. A SweeteningOrFlavouringQuality class is introduced to discriminate sweetened/flavoured water from plain water, but this new quality class needs to be introduced. Coverage is partial due to the missing disjointness with heading 2009 juices and the absence of a formal OWL representation of the Chapter 22 Note 3 definitional boundary.
